TSLA: How Lucid Went From Near-Term Upside to Major Disappointment. Lucid Group, despite launching its Gravity SUV and having an opportunity to gain market share from Tesla, continues to face significant production and supplier issues. A recent recall due to a rear-seat defect and a widened net loss of $1 billion in the first quarter have dashed investor hopes. The company has suspended full-year production guidance, implemented a 12% workforce reduction to conserve cash, and is now focusing on a significant production ramp-up in 2027.

Tesla Solar Roof is on life support as it pivot to panels. Tesla's Solar Roof, once hailed as revolutionary, has largely failed to meet its ambitious targets, with Tesla installing only about 3,000 systems since its 2016 launch. The company is now quietly shifting its focus to conventional solar panels, leaving existing Solar Roof customers with significant service issues and unfulfilled promises. This strategic pivot highlights the economic challenges and deployment failures of the Solar Roof product compared to traditional solar panel solutions.
TGT: Branchout Food Q1 2026 Earnings Call: Complete Transcript. Branchout Food (NASDAQ: BOF) held its first-quarter earnings conference call, detailing strategies for increased factory utilization and customer expansion through partnerships with major retailers like Costco, Sam's Club, Walmart, and Target. The company is investing in inventory buildup for a record Q2, exploring a tolling opportunity for drying services, and projects significant revenue growth in the second half of 2026 with improved liquidity from recent financing.

Trillium urges Target (TGT) shareholders to oppose Executive Chair Cornell, Lead Director Leahy. Trillium Asset Management has filed an exempt solicitation urging Target (TGT) shareholders to vote against Executive Chair Brian Cornell and Lead Independent Director Christine Leahy at the upcoming June 10, 2026 Annual Meeting. Trillium cites Target's multi-year underperformance against the S&P 500 Consumer Discretionary Index, recurring sales declines, reputational damage, and governance concerns regarding the decision to retain Cornell as Executive Chair after promoting an insider to CEO. The letter highlights significant share repurchases that failed to boost stock price and substantial compensation for Mr. Cornell.

Target Corp stock (US8725901040): shares react to latest quarterly update. The article discusses Target Corp's business model, key revenue drivers, and its significance for US investors following its latest quarterly update. It highlights the retailer's mix of essential and discretionary categories, its increasing reliance on digital and same-day services, and its role as an indicator of broader consumer spending trends. The analysis emphasizes how Target balances competitive pricing with profitability and focuses on store-based fulfillment, reflecting current shifts in household budgets and market dynamics.
SBUX: Starbucks Wins Fifth Circuit Challenge to NLRB Firing Ruling. Starbucks Corp. has won its appeals court challenge against a National Labor Relations Board (NLRB) ruling regarding the alleged illegal firing of a worker involved in union activities. The Fifth Circuit Court of Appeals vacated the NLRB's decision, sending the case back for further review, citing that the board did not adequately consider contradictory evidence. The case concerns a shift supervisor dismissed from a Starbucks near Albany, N.Y., who was involved in organizing efforts.

Capital World Investors owns 104.8M SBUX shares (NASDAQ: SBUX). Capital World Investors has reported beneficial ownership of 104,838,568 shares of Starbucks Corp. (NASDAQ: SBUX), which represents 9.2% of the company's believed outstanding shares. This disclosure was made via an amended Schedule 13G/A filing signed on May 13, 2026. The filing attributes sole voting power to 103,833,219 shares and sole dispositive power to all 104,838,568 shares.
DIS: Phunware names new CEO, taps Disney+ veteran for AI platform push. Phunware Inc. has appointed Dmitry Kroshka as its new CEO and engaged product consultancy Build Something Product Group, led by former Disney+ executive Michael Cerda, to advance its "2.0 Strategy." This strategy focuses on converting the company's mobile, location-aware, and customer engagement capabilities into a unified, AI-enabled platform for large-property operators in hospitality and healthcare. The company aims to sharpen its commercial focus on "Guest Intelligence" and has seen strong engagement with its AI Concierge module.

Disney Springs Columbia Store Set for Major Remodel. The Columbia Sportswear store at Disney Springs is scheduled for a major refurbishment, as indicated by a recently filed Notice of Commencement. The remodel plans include updates to the storefront, lighting, wall finishes, fixtures, and paint for the 5,000-square-foot location. It is currently unknown whether the store will close during the renovation period.

Tracy McGrady's OBL lands on Fubo Sports Network in first streaming deal. Tracy McGrady's Ones Basketball League (OBL) has secured its first streaming deal with Fubo Sports Network for its Summer 2026 "OBL: Battle of The Cities" season. The agreement will bring one-on-one basketball matchups featuring city-based teams with celebrity owners to a streaming audience for the first time, starting May 15, 2026. This partnership expands Fubo Sports Network's content offerings and leverages the growing Free Ad-Supported Streaming TV (FAST) market, especially after FuboTV's merger with Disney's Hulu + Live TV.

Dana Walden talks future of Disney. Dana Walden, President and Chief Creative Officer of The Walt Disney Company, discussed Disney's expansive future plans in an interview with "Good Morning America." She highlighted upcoming films like "The Devil Wears Prada 2" and "Toy Story 5," praised Disney's streaming services for housing beloved content, and previewed major live events such as the Super Bowl and the Grammys, along with new initiatives for younger audiences, including a Bluey feature at Disneyland. Walden emphasized Disney's commitment to high-quality emotional storytelling and its success in partnering with creators.
NKE: Nike Tariff Lawsuit Adds Pressure To Margins And Investor Confidence. Nike is facing a proposed class-action lawsuit alleging unlawful tariff-related pricing practices, claiming the company passed illegal costs to consumers without refunds after tariffs were struck down. This lawsuit adds to existing pressures on Nike's profitability and margins, raising concerns for investors about future pricing, potential settlements, and the company's brand perception. The outcome could impact Nike's direct-to-consumer strategy and margin repair efforts, as well as influencing how it communicates government-related charges and structures future price increases.

Shoe Carnival Inc stock (US8322481093): Family footwear retailer with value focus. Shoe Carnival Inc (SCVL) is a US family footwear retailer operating over 400 stores in 35 states, offering value-priced shoes, apparel, and accessories from brands like Nike and Skechers. The company targets budget-conscious families and relies significantly on physical sales, with an e-commerce platform contributing 10-15% of revenue. Its business model emphasizes a fun shopping experience, private labels, and strategic partnerships, providing investors exposure to consumer discretionary spending in the Midwest and Southern US.
